output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? How to Recover Your Money Fast
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights, what landlords can legally deduct, and how to write a demand letter that gets results.
content_outline: Opening Hook: When the Landlord Won't Give Your Deposit Back
Can a Landlord Keep Your Security Deposit? (Know the Legal Limits)
What Landlords Can and Cannot Deduct From Your Deposit
Tenant Rights: How Long a Landlord Has to Return Your Deposit
What to Do When Your Landlord Is Not Returning Your Deposit
How to Write a Security Deposit Dispute Letter That Works
Escalation Tactics If Your Deposit Is Still Not Returned
FAQ: Addressing 'My Landlord Won't Give Me My Deposit Back'
key_points: Landlords must return security deposits within a state-specific timeframe, typically 14 to 30 days.
Normal wear and tear, unpaid rent, and excessive damage are the only categories where deductions may be legal.
A formal security deposit dispute letter is the most effective first step before filing in small claims court.
Document all communication, lease clauses, and move-out photos to strengthen your recovery case.
output.cta: Stop waiting for a landlord who won't return your deposit. Use DepositBack to generate a legally sound, state-specific security deposit demand letter in minutes—and put pressure on your landlord to pay up.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters who are problem-aware, have had their security deposit withheld or not returned, and are actively searching for legal next steps and demand letter solutions.
